hotel_name: Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, Curio Collection by Hilton

reviews.title: Worst experience staying in  Vegas
reviews.text: Very bad hotel envinronment

reviews.title: Constant noise and repeated staff interruptions made it impossible to sleep, leaving us exhausted for our six-hour drive
reviews.positives: Easy check-in.
reviews.negatives:
Our stay was extremely disappointing. We were assigned a room on the 11th floor, and unfortunately, we barely got any sleep due to constant noise from the elevator brakes throughout the night. It was loud enough to keep both my husband and me awake.

The following morning only made things worse. Despite being told at check-in that checkout was at noon, the cleaning staff repeatedly attempted to enter our room early, which woke us up again. There was no functioning “Do Not Disturb” sign available, which is something I’ve never experienced at any hotel before. I even tried to signal that we were still inside, but it didn’t work. I ended up having to use the top lock to prevent anyone from coming in.

Overall, we left feeling exhausted and had to drive six hours back home in that condition, which made the experience even more frustrating. This stay did not meet basic expectations for rest and privacy.

reviews.title: Worst experience I have ever had
reviews.text:
First of  all,  a room with a broken light system was given to me. I had to request a room change but it was fully book that day, they claimed. 

Second of all, the new room I got assigned to was a downgraded room with smaller space, facing another room and no bathtub while my original room was obviously larger and facing the city of Las Vegas. 

If you encounter this room change, you will probably expect some sort of refunds right? But NO! THEY DON"T CARE. NO REFUNDS, NO FOOD AND BEVERAGE VOUCHER, NO ANYTHING. And I even have to move my luggage myself after a day with exhausting body and soul at near mid-night.

I highly DON"T recommend Virgin Hotel if you are planning to visit Las Vegas. It will only bring you horrible travel experience.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: Bad service … they advertise as pet friendly hotel … they saw me get into the facilities and check in with my cat clearly visible .. and they called me 2 days later at 11 pm in the middle of the night to  demand to take out my pet because they are only dog friendly. My pet was no causing any damage or disturbing. But they say I was breaking their policies by having a cat . And I had to check out immediately.  Very disturbing situation an horrible management. Why did they let me check in in first place when they saw my cat getting in with me ?

They just ruin our mood at the end of our trip nd the lady on the desk is so rude she don't know how to reply the guests . And strip view room is not have strip view its just pool view room .

Please know that we strive to maintain transparency, and all pricing and accommodation details are listed online to help our guests make informed decisions. We truly appreciate your feedback, and we hope you’ll consider giving us another opportunity in the future to provide you with a more positive and enjoyable experience.

reviews.title: Filthy
reviews.positives: Parking was free
reviews.negatives:
Here’s your updated review incorporating that detail:

I recently booked a two-night stay at the Virgin Hotel, but I had to check out at 6 AM on the first day due to completely unacceptable room conditions. The room was filthy—dog hair covered the couch and other areas, there were stains on the walls, and I even found a used water bottle under the bed. The air quality was so bad that I couldn’t breathe properly or get any sleep.

When I requested a room change, the hotel informed me they were fully booked and couldn’t move me until later. Staying another night was not an option, and I wasn’t about to wait around in a dirty room for hours hoping for a new one.

To make matters worse, I never even received a receipt via email—just a survey. It’s been two weeks, and I still haven’t gotten my money back for the night I didn’t stay. I reported this to Booking.com a week ago, and I have yet to hear back from either the hotel or Booking.com.

The lack of communication and accountability is beyond frustrating. I do not recommend this hotel, and I’m extremely disappointed in both Virgin Hotel and Booking.com for their poor customer service. If you value cleanliness, responsiveness, and basic hospitality, stay somewhere else.

 If you’re just looking for a cheap place to stay, it may work—but don’t expect the quality or service of a typical Hilton property.

reviews.title: Front Desk Staff
reviews.text:
Im not one to complain. But the check in line was really long when we arrived at 12NN. So we left our luggage and checked in at night instead. 

Got there around 1030PM and there were 2 girls Gaby and Monica in the front desk, chatting, giggling , taking their time as we wait in line for a good 30mins! There were only 4 people in front of me. When it was my turn, Monica said she couldnt find my Agoda booking. At that point I said my phone died and if she could search using my name. She said no.  I had to find a way to charge my phone and when it did, I went to thr other counter Gaby. She was annoyed that I distubed their chatting.  I said can you try to look for my booking? She said why didnt she (Monica) help you? I said she couldnt find it so can you try on your end? Very snarkily she said what makes you think I can if she cant?

Her manager/supervisor told her to go ahead and taught her how to navigate and they were able to find my reservation!

Thank you to the supervisor but the other staff needs training.

reviews.title: If you only want the guest's money

reviews.title: Things to be aware of prior to booking
reviews.text:
- Check in time was 3pm but we did not get 3 out of 5 rooms till after 5pm. When we spoke to reception about the delay they said they will provide $50 credit per room for the inconvenience, however did not explain that this $50 would not be deducted from the total bill/ resorts fees instead it was credit to be used at the hotel facilities. This meant we did not use the total credit. 
- No tea/coffee making items in rooms 
- Minibar and fridge in rooms are motion detected so if an item is even lifted up and placed back down, it is charged to the room, which is quite an inconvenience because then reception will have to remove items from the bill 
- Hidden costs of resort fees of $56 a day
reviews.response:
Dear Munasinghege,
Thank you for your detailed feedback. We're glad you chose to stay with us, but we understand your frustration with the challenges you faced during your stay. We want to apologize for the inconvenience at check-in and will address this with our team. Transparency is vital to us, and our resort charges are listed on our website. The in-room minibar has instructions on its operation. If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out to our team. We appreciate your comments and hope to welcome you back for a smoother stay on your next visit to Las Vegas.

reviews.title: Safety, cleanliness, privacy and billing failures
reviews.positives: Generally unpleasant stay.
reviews.negatives:
Stayed 5 nights in total. The room looked fine at first, but issues escalated quickly. The in-room phone barely worked and had no instructions. A personal item went missing (no conclusion reached), and service requests were slow and incomplete.

We later found a fresh piece of blue chewing gum on the floor next to the bed, which raised serious cleanliness and safety concerns. Despite using Do Not Disturb, the room was entered. The next night, we arrive back in the hotel room and we have a water bottle knocked over on the floor with the lid detached-it was not done by us. And when we got into bed, a pillow had a large, cold wet patch with no visible cause, which was extremely unsettling. We filed security report and explained the situation to at least 5 different staff. They told us they would investigate but we haven’t heard anything from them until now, weeks later, and we were not allowed to have a copy of the security report we filed. We still don’t know what exactly happened. We were anxious for a few nights and not able to sleep well.

We moved rooms late at night in the final night and were promised waived resort fees and a complimentary late checkout. At checkout, we were still charged, including fees that were supposed to be waived—and we were overcharged extra resort fees for a night where the resort fee was prepaid online. Two weeks later, accounting has not responded to our concerns. 

Would not recommend.

reviews.title: Could be awesome
reviews.text: I was excited to stay here. Valet attendant yelled at me for not pulling up a couple car lengths more (even though it wasn’t busy), then creepily told me how the hotel doesn’t charge guests for valet, it’s all tips, “so if you take care of me I’ll take care of you.” It was threatening and I feared for my belongings I left inside as he drove my car off without an opening tip. Checked in at 4 pm, went straight down to the pool and it closed at 4 pm.  Rooms are spacious, and there are a lot of rooms here.  Might try it again as I used to love Virgin airlines.
reviews.response:
Dear Darren,
Thank you for sharing your recent experience. While we're pleased our spacious accommodations was a highlight of your visit, we sincerely regret that you were not fully satisfied. Your feedback about the service you received will be discussed internally with our team to ensure our high standards of hospitality are being met and to seize any opportunity to improve. However, we do want you to know we have strict policies in place to ensure our guests’ belongings are not tampered with by team members. It was a pleasure having you as our guest, and we hope you enjoyed your time in Las Vegas.

reviews.title: Hotel on strike!
reviews.negatives: No notification from booking.com or Virgin Hotel regarding employee strike!  Picketers outside property, had bullhorns at night, all night. Short staffed, exceptional long waits for front desk service.  Call before staying at this property, would not recommend until hotel strike is over!

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: House cleaning was bad, I found shocks under the bed when I check in. The pillow was filled with small hairs. Front desk staff was not helpful in checking in.

reviews.title: Unreasonable charges for unclear things
reviews.negatives: They took unreasonable charges at the begging. $300 as a deposit is unheard of. Plus $100 that is nonrefundable for who knows what. The desk worker was very rude when we asked what was the money for and if we still need to pay for the amenities they counted the money towards. She said “Obviously yes.” So till this day it is a mystery why they charged that money. The room was nice but it’s very loud outside and you can hear everything.

reviews.title: Average hotel though 4 star
reviews.text: Carpark is free , not too far away. Have to remember to close toilet door while bathing. My kids didn’t and it triggered the fire alarm, which we find it quite stressful.
reviews.response:
Dear Julie,
Thank you for sharing your experience. It’s great to hear that our free parking and prime location added to your stay, and we appreciate you mentioning this to your fellow travelers. That said, we understand how the fire alarm being triggered could have been stressful, especially with kids. Your feedback is helpful, and we’ll look into ways to better inform guests to avoid such situations. We appreciate your honesty and hope to welcome you back soon.

reviews.title: Don't stay here if you have dogs.
reviews.positives: The decor of the room and restaurant options in the casino below. Also, front-desk staff were very kind and helpful after two pitbulls tried to attack my small dogs at check-in.
reviews.negatives: The pictures for this place really promote it as a 'dog-friendly' hotel. As someone who was only traveling "thru" Vegas instead of "to," I just needed a place to stay that would be a cost effective option with dogs. The only pet-relief area was a dirty patch of astro-turf in the 115 degree heat by the parking garage that required I walk my two dogs through the entire length of the busy casino every time they needed to go out. The parking lot, while free, is also a huge hassle if you have pets, as it is at the opposite end of the hotel to guest check-in and an oven when temps are high. The hotel is advertised as this beautiful and peaceful oasis, going so far as to charge a $45 resort fee. IT'S A CASINO. A loud, smoky, run of the mill casino that you have to walk thru to get to any of the 'resort' amenities like the pool or the spa. My room was nice -- really love the style, but the bathroom felt dirty -- there were hairs in the shower and a few left in the sink. Made me question the rest of the room's cleanliness. It wasn't for me.

reviews.title: strike
reviews.text: It would have been nice to know that a major strike was going on there.  Also, the bothroom (toilet and shower) area were VERY small, you had to back up into the toilet to shut the door to the room, or the shower door, and could not open both at the same time.  Other than that, it was fine.

reviews.title: $450 a night in a room with no view
reviews.negatives:
The check-in at the front desk was very poor experience and the staff was unwelcoming and unhelpful. 
For a room of $450 a night, I was expecting some kind of view instead of a white wall and HVOC units.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: Not too family friendly. No fridge/microwave in the room. Pool closes early.

reviews.title: Bad service by front desk
reviews.text: Service was nil and very sour once the front of desk staff failed to upsell. Requested for high floor but was given the lowest possible floor when occupancy was not even high. Bed type was not guaranteed as well as we booked for a double bed via agoda but was given twin bed.

reviews.title: The walkway to the room is very far
reviews.text: The walkway to the room is very far.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: i found the bed in my room was too firm and uncomfortable. the soundproofing quality is also bad as i could hear noise from the adjoining room shared door. the room extra's, such as coffee for brewing, were not provided, as was the stationary and pen that is usually standard.

reviews.title: loud airport noise
reviews.text: its near the airport, you hear all the planes taking off and landing
reviews.response:
Dear Sean,
We appreciate your feedback regarding your recent stay. We regret that our location did not fully meet your expectations. Our team hopes you enjoyed your visit to Las Vegas, and we wish you safe travels.

reviews.title: Thin walls! You can literally hear your neighbors breathe
reviews.text: Pools are close in winter and thin walls need to be said. There is a fun and funky vibe through the resort and things were clean. Staff are friendly
reviews.response:
Dear Corinne,
Thank you for your feedback. We're glad you enjoyed our resort's fun vibe and our high standards of service and cleanliness. We've also noted your concerns about sound and seasonal pool access and appreciate you sharing your experience. We hope to have an opportunity to welcome you back for an even more enjoyable visit.

reviews.title: Visited Vegas in a group of 4
reviews.text: The room was clean, but only two small water bottles were provided for drinking. There was no microwave and kettle too. A resort fee of 54 dollars was charged with no additional services

reviews.title: Lacking in Room Appliances
reviews.text: No microwave nor coffee-maker,.not even a hot water kettle

reviews.title: Good but dusty
reviews.text: Same as title
reviews.response:
Dear Suhyeon,
Thank you for your review. We apologize for any aspect of your room that was less than perfect and will mention this to our housekeeping team. We hope you enjoyed the rest of your visit and time in Las Vegas. Come back again soon to experience a stay worthy of a perfect rating. Until next time.

reviews.title: Rooms great, Casino sad
reviews.text: Casino dead a f doesn't pay.  No drink service.

reviews.title: Staff difficult to communicate and it has a resort fee with a rather desolated venue
reviews.positives: Room is clean and it’s location is not very far from everything else
reviews.negatives: The property can’t even provide a post stay invoice. And there’s a tray / table in the room with the bar - be super careful with that because apparently even if you just moved anything, they charge you.

reviews.title: Not the best stay, you’re better off being closer to the strip.
reviews.text: The hotel is a 5 min drive from the strip or 25 minute walk. They only give you two bottles of water a day and no electric kettle to make your own water. They also charged a daily resort fee of $59, I guess this is for the two bottles of water?! During checkout there was this random resort charge of $198, which I asked them to remove because the staff didn’t know what it was either.

reviews.title: Nor worth the price
reviews.positives: Fast wifi
reviews.negatives:
1. Room was too far from the lobby
2. Poor cleaning
3. Too noisy at night

reviews.title: Do Better!
reviews.text: Though the staff were nice; the facilities were subpar! Couldn’t use luggage cart without a bellboy and pulling heavy luggage on carpet way on the other side of the building is hard! Carpet needs to be shampooed! Pool DJ starts at 9:00am and you can hear him all day from your room. Door between rooms has no outer lining, so you can also hear the people in the next room like they’re in your room! The staff was nice enough to move me to the other side…But the inconveniences weren’t worth it!

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Design, colors, materials
reviews.negatives: Cleanliness is far from too notch. Service is sub par at the very best, disappointing

reviews.title: Just OK, nothing stands out
reviews.text: Very busy place with  lots going on, check in staffs were alright, but check in process and check out process super slow. Hotel staffs especially at restaurants and cafes were quite rude, even when we order the coffee to drink, we were told we couldn't sit at the place and we need to eat to be able to sit there which was a meh (what's going on? - we are guests, not mere stranger and it was not that we didn't order anything?). Didn't leave us great impression. Will be finding elsewhere next time in Vegas
reviews.response:
Dear Phuong,
Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We appreciate your feedback regarding your recent stay. We strive to provide a welcoming atmosphere for all our guests, and we regret this was not reflected during your visit. Your comments are valuable to us, and we will address them with our team to ensure improvements are made. We hope to have the opportunity in the future to ensure an improved experience.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: No coffee machine, No useful space  in the refrigerator. Receptionist didn't send me the room ready message that he promised. In room check out  procedure looks incomplete so I went to lobby to confirm my check out with receptionist.

reviews.title: Overall is good, one of the staffs was rude and unhelpful
reviews.text:
Was told by Serena (not sure if spelled correctly) during checking in ar 3pm that we don’t have our room ready yet and she has no ETA for the room to be ready.  She definitely ruined the team. 

Overall is great
reviews.response:
Dear Lau,
Thank you for your review. It was a pleasure having you join us during your time in Las Vegas; however, we apologize for the wait you encountered for your room and for any disappointment with the service you received. We'll be sure to discuss your feedback it nearly to ensure our high standards of hospitality are being met. We hope to welcome you back in the future for a seamless stay worthy of a perfect rating.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: The elevators didn’t work consistently and the toilet ran the ENTIRE TIME

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Restaurants are rip off

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Clearness and service
reviews.negatives: Bathroom too small for me due to my disability.

reviews.title: Nice hotel off the strip but...
reviews.text: It's a really nice hotel with a nice pool. But it's off the strip so there's very little going on within walking distance. Rooms are clean. But there's no kettle provided within the room so you can't make your own coffee/tea. But what's even odder is that the housekeeping staff have no qualms entering your room with their master key after just knocking once or twice... even when we were asleep! This happened three times during our one-week stay.
reviews.response:
Dear Jonas,
Thank you for your review and complimenting our beautiful pool and clean accommodations. However, we apologize for any concerns with our location or housekeeping. Your feedback has been noted, and we will speak with our housekeeping team regarding your comments to ensure we deliver only the best service. We are delighted you chose to stay with us, and we hope you will consider a return visit the next time you are in Las Vegas.

reviews.title: Not enough staff to address adequate customer service needs
reviews.positives: It was a nice trip until we saw they charged us duplicate & now they won’t respond.
reviews.negatives: We were prepaid with booking.com but they charged our credit card a whole new nightly rate of $400 erroneously and now we cannot get anyone to reply to fix this. Very disappointing for a trip to unwind.

reviews.title: Nice Hotel But A Bit Pricey!
reviews.text: I used to love Hard Rock Hotel but Virgin Hotels isn't the same. Suites are a bit too pricey but the establishment otherwise I'd come back again.
reviews.response:

While our pricing is competitive with the market, our aim is to provide value through our services and amenities, and we will take your comments into account as we continually strive to meet our guests' expectations. Thank you for sharing your experience, and we look forward to the opportunity to welcome you back.

Room shown that they did cleaning but not that clean as it still has some dust on sofa, wall and some corner.

They do daily make up your room but they try to get in your room (do knock and ring the bell) even we put the do not disturb.

reviews.negatives: View. And cleanness and update on the facility is needed

reviews.title: Good hotel

reviews.title: Disappointing…

reviews.negatives: This hotel is overpriced, with hidden charges.. I’m not coming back to this hotel…tiny bathroom/ washroom

reviews.text: Overall, hotel is nice and the location is exceptional for being off strip. However, we returned home  and found multiple bed bug bites across our bodies.

reviews.positives: Spacious and clean
reviews.negatives: Staff and this disconnected nature of multiple operators. The hotel is one entity, casino another and pool staff is another, therefore when you had a problem it was never the hotel management’s responsibility.

A bit far from LV strip if you don’t plan to drive around.

reviews.title: If you’re looking for a reasonably priced hotel awsy from the nightlife of LV. This hotel works.  Poorly staffed - long
reviews.positives: It was competitively priced
reviews.negatives: When you book with a third party expect to be given the worst rooms in your booking class.   Service was poor

reviews.title: Affordable non-stripe hotel
reviews.text: If you want to stay at an affordable, non-bustling stripe hotel, Virgin Hotel will do.

reviews.title: The hotel is nice and clean with modern interior but we were disappointed with the lost items . We'v
reviews.positives: I like the cleanliness, free parking, not too crowded casino.
reviews.negatives: There's no refrigerator we can put our drinks in. There was no ice for 4 days. What I didn't like was one our bags(Macy's reusable shopping bag )  with sneakers was missing. We stayed for 5 nights and when I was looking to wear that pair, we can't find it anywhere.

reviews.title: Don't recommend it as a place to live
reviews.text:
The location is perfectly nice for those who need to get to the airport, even without taking a taxi or driving. The room looks nice with everything included.
However, the key point is, it's super uncomfortable to rest. The showerhead worked badly and the water out of it is thing and small, which made me very cold. The pillow is way too high and soft, which makes me sick. The quilt is thin and hard to pull. I am too cold to fall asleep during the first half of the night and kept having nightmares. The last half of the night I increased the temperature of the air condition and felt too hot as well. It might be the worst sleep I have ever had. Also, it's not so easy to call the front desk when I'm in the room. I haven't tried other facilities, but I had a fever immediately after leaving this place. When I tried to borrow a thermometer from the front desk, they told me there wasn't one. Wow, then why don't you mention that the hotel offered a daily temperature check for each staff? Is that a lie?
For those who want to have a good sleep, please avoid this hotel.

reviews.title: Comfortable but high “resort fee”
reviews.text: If you’re booking this for the price, beware that when you arrive you’ll be hit with a $56 per night “resort fee.” We asked what resort amenities this includes and they said the pool and spa. In January the pool was closed and you have to buy a day pass to enjoy the spa. So yeah. Otherwise it’s clean and comfortable. Not an exciting scene and you will need Ubers to get to the strip. (Adding to the cost) So while it is a much better budget option than some of the scary/depressing off-strip properties, it  might be worth spending the extra to stay on the strip.
reviews.response:
Dear Regina,
Thank you for sharing your detailed feedback. It's great to hear that you found your accommodations clean and comfortable during your stay. While our resort fee information is readily available on our website and across third-party sites, we regret any inconvenience it may have caused. Your comments are valuable, and we will share them with our team to help ensure future guests enjoy a more seamless stay.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Rooms are really nice.Food prices are ridiculous and they need more places to eat.
reviews.response:
Dear Pasquale,
Thank you for sharing your feedback; it’s wonderful that you enjoyed our comfortable rooms. That being said, we understand how pricing and dining variety can shape a stay, and we appreciate the opportunity to clarify. Our property features 12 dining options, each showcasing distinct culinary styles and flavors to cater to a diverse range of preferences. Pricing reflects the use of high-quality ingredients and the care our chefs take in preparing each dish. We’re glad you enjoyed your visit overall and look forward to welcoming you back for another enjoyable stay.

reviews.title: Dirty bed sheets
reviews.text: The bed sheet has hair and spots, it looks so bad
reviews.response:
Dear Eddie,
Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We’re truly sorry your stay didn’t reflect the care and quality we’re known for, and please accept our sincere apology. What you described is not typical, and our housekeeping team is addressing this immediately to ensure it doesn’t happen again. We’re committed to maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness and would love the opportunity to provide you with a much more comfortable and seamless stay in the future.

reviews.title: Overall it was ok
reviews.text:
The hotel location is not walking distance to much around so prepare to spend on rideshares. The hotel has a cool decor and the room was big and spacious. The bathroom is a big space and has barely space to put toiletries. The bathroom needs shelves and more hooks or racks to hang towels. The closet space is very small. I didn’t appreciate that there’s sensors in the fridge that charge you if you move something. I only know this info because i asked if i could move things in the mini bar to put some things in the fridge and then i was told about the censors. A room should have a fridge that i can use to store things too. 
Front desk at check in was not very welcoming so that needs work. 
I also had an issue at the spa- make sure you register everyone in your room so you get the hotel spa guest pass price. This is also an error on the front desk person at check in because she only registered 1 person to the room.  The manager resolved this later on so it worked out. 
The spa is very basic with steam, sauna, jacuzzi and another coed pool with lounge space. The women’s lounge area furniture is nice but not cozy enough for spa lounge and the fireplace feature was not working. Also, the jacuzzi area- it needs some attention. Missing mosaic tiles, old grout, it did not look good and the lounge cushions in the coed pool need replacing asap. 
Restaurants are decent- the mexican restaurant seemed to have more traffic than the others and the breakfast restaurant had a decent buffet- very basic with fruits, pastries (toast is not included)eggs, potatoes, bacon, and omelette station. 
The pool area was nice but not enough free sun loungers. 

This hotel needs to offer a shuttle to the strip. 
I don’t know if i would stay here again mostly because of the location .
reviews.response:
Dear Miriam,
We're glad you enjoyed your time at our pool, our delicious breakfast offerings, and our wonderful rooms while you were here. However, we apologize for any disappointing service or any other concerns you had during your visit. Rest assured, we will take your comments into consideration as we continuously strive to make improvements for the benefit of our great guests. It was a pleasure having you here, and we hope to have another opportunity to go above and beyond for you in the future.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: The location was great, no charge for parking, tons of options for food. The pool was beautiful. The only thing I was disappointed in was the upgrade.  I thought the tub was going to be  bigger.  In the AC seemed to stop cooling as good in the morning we were leaving
reviews.response:
Dear Aireal,
Thank you for your review. It’s great to hear that our convenient location, complimentary parking, and dining variety enhanced your stay, along with the enjoyment of our pool area. Your concerns about your tub size and the air conditioning performance are appreciated and will be taken into consideration as part of ongoing efforts to improve guest comfort and satisfaction. We hope to welcome you back for a seamless experience on your next visit.

reviews.title: Loud
reviews.text:
There is concert every Saturday in the lawn and most reina face this área, so it’s loud. 
Also the 3 pools close esa toy at 6:30pm. Overpriced food but that seems to be the norm in Las Vegas nowadays . For go the dunkin in there because it’s twice expensive to the Dukin across the street

reviews.title: Not health friendly
reviews.text: Ruby towers are great because they have their own registration and parking lot, fast elevators. The room is beautiful with a deep bathtub! What is hate is they try to get as much money out of you as possible by a refrigerator that's full of drinks, a tray on the counter with snacks, they all set to charge you if anything is removed from their spot! Gives me unnecessary anxiety when I'm here to relax, no coffee maker or anything to heat tea! The fact that a new hotel need to try to corner you for more money instead of a relax-free environment are deplorable! And the worse part are the restaurants! UNHEALTHY foods! Mostly fried! If that's not your poison let's try scoops of sugar on meat the orange chicken might as well be a cake! All the salt was like eating a pretzel all day everyday! Seniors with health issues might as well consider fasting if they want to live past checkout! I'm 70 and with the price they charge for rooms in want freedom of choice, not force to a slow demise from forcing me to eat, drink what they only have by no refrigeration, no heating of foods and UNHEALTHY restaurants! But a great place if you're fasting or 20 years old, but unfortunately most 20 years old people couldn't afford the room. 😑😤 to sum it all up, its a beautiful room but all around not worth the money!

reviews.title: Beautiful Casino, some issues though.
reviews.text: We enjoyed our stay. It wasn’t too far from the strip which was nice and had better rates because of that. Unfortunately there’s a strike going on there which we didn’t know about so it’s extremely loud arriving to the hotel. We also didn’t have hot water for half the stay which was frustrating and nothing was offered as compensation for this. The restaurants were nice, except be warned if you have anyone under 21 you can’t go to half of them. Rooms were a nice size and felt clean.

reviews.title: We can't recommend it
reviews.positives: Room illumination was good - a lot of options
reviews.negatives: 1. We had a door to another room right in front of the bed. I mean another room - other hotel's guests. A lot of noise and hard to sleep - you can hear every word of your neighbour. 2. The fridge was full of stuff, and you couldn't put your stuff there, so it was useless. 3. The size of the water bottles was a joke

reviews.title: Disgusting
reviews.text: Rather newer looking hotel. But they did not advertise that there would be some disturbance from protesting people outside their hotel, which has happened for a while apparently. Upon cancellation request, they won't refund my money hence I had to bear with it. Yes I couldn't hear it from the room, but can hear them in reception area, parking area and few other places. And hotel was pretty quiet due to that. Room is quite spacious and bright which was great. But when I got into the bathroom, there were rubbish from the previous guest in the shower as well as human debris in it. I am wondering if they have issues with staffing, as per the protestor. Not the best experience unfortunately. Will I come back?Honestly I don't mind if they improve the cleanliness and no more protesting.

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.positives: It was ok for one quick night that we had to stay in Vegas because close to airport. And had restaurants on site.
reviews.negatives: Our room was filthy. Finger prints visible everywhere. Bathroom smelled like pee and mold.

reviews.text: I had to stay another night before leaving Vegas after the tour, so I only slept, but the design was nice, and the room was spacious and clean. It's far from the city center, so I wouldn't recommend it for those who want to go downtown.

reviews.title: It was ok

reviews.negatives: The staff member who checked me in was quite unfriendly and not helpful. It started my stay on a sour note. The resort fee is ridiculous for what you get.

It’s not far off from the strip and parking is free. The room is spacious and the beds were comfortable. 

Cons: Refrigerator holds only minibar items, AC vent is noisy and blows directly at the bed, and water in the shower doesn’t drain properly.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The pools were really night, but one of them was closed. The pool bar was also nice. The rooms were overall modern designed and we were able to see some of the main strip from our window, which I also appreciated. Once we complained the first night, they did accommodate us by placing us in a different room.
reviews.negatives: The beds had sheets as comforters, not thick enough at all for comfort. Most of the ice machines didn’t work, and the one that did was very dirty, all of them had expired service stickers on them. The location of our room from the actual entrance of the hotel/lobby was a very far walk. The front desk lines were always long and the staff was always short. After our flight was delayed to Vegas, we arrived late, which wasn’t their fault, but our room was not cleaned very well when we arrived, it was a bad first impression. The “sand” at one of the pools was so hot, that it burned my feet after just trying to get to the pool water, which was less than a couple of feet away. On top of everything, the price of anything in the convenience store was absurdly expensive. A bottle water was more than $8; it was not very convenient when we had to take a trip to the CVS across the street for the basics, it was supposed to be a RELAXING VACATION. Will definitely be staying somewhere else next time we visit.

reviews.title: Resort Fee?!
reviews.text: The price was double what was [initially] advertised.  I asked and they all shied away from the questions. I was not going to ruin their Christmas as they did to my pocket/wallet.  Scam!  Stay away.

reviews.title: A $54 daily resort fee sucks along with a $45 candy bar fee .

reviews.title: good deal, but beware of extra charges

reviews.title: I was not satisfied with my stay at this location. Unsure if I’ll be trying this hotel again or recommending it to anyon

reviews.negatives: I was given an optional for a tub or shower. And I didn’t get what I opt for also I paid for a view of the Strip that I did not get.  The elevators were not working & there were no signs saying so. The check-in staff was extremely rude & seemed irritable as if she did not want to be at work. I ask for a manager or supervisor and never heard from one during my stay

reviews.title: Beautiful Room
reviews.text: We were thrilled with the room we chose (Ruby Chamber King Corner), but were less impressed with the housekeeping staff. We never had a visit the first two days, and had to ask multiple times to have the rooms made up. Not what I would expect from a higher end hotel.

reviews.title: Lovely bed zero vibes experience no pool
reviews.positives: The bed and the manager. The staff is lovely I feel bad they have to work here….. It used to be one of the best now it’s on par to bring one of the worst.
reviews.negatives:
There are no fun pools open in the winter?! This used to be the Hard Rock which was Awesome! Now it’s a corporate drag space for uniformed puppets to parade around at some convention! Zero vibe!!!!! reviews.title: Looks fancy from the outside but feels flawed on the inside.

reviews.negatives: The parking is technically free, but it’s a hassle — a long walk and two elevators to get to the room. There were only three towels for four people, which seems ridiculous. The windows don’t open, and the air conditioning blasts, with no way to avoid it except turning it off completely. The fridge was filled with unnecessary paid items, and if you want to use it for your own things, you’ll be charged $40 — a bizarre setup. The light switches are fancy, but they work poorly and make it hard to use them — regular switches would have been much better. The walls are paper-thin, and I could hear neighbors talking and coughing during the night.

The minibar had some stuff in it, so I couldn't use it to chill our champagne we brought. If you move any items in there - you get charged. If you want the minibar emptied - they can do that, but for a $40 charge. 
At the checkout the staff didn't even ask how was our stay.

reviews.title: Pretty Good Overall
reviews.text: The hotel is well situated, but off the strip. This means that you will need to ride share to most places on the strip, however it is not very expensive to do so (10-12 dollars per ride on average). The hotel rooms are nice and do not require a lot of cleaning. Do note that if you require extra towels or whatever, you will need to wait a while because the property is huge. Initially, it was difficult getting in contact with the front desk to change rooms due to the first one needing cleaning, however the new room was great and the phone worked perfectly. Like any Vegas hotel, they will nickel and dime you so make sure you are aware of any potential purchases you may be making by asking for the cost prior to ordering/requesting anything. Do also note that you should not take anything out of the fridge as it automatically charges you even if you do not consume the beverage, but it states that on a sticker on the fridge. Overall, solid hotel and I would give it a 4/5.
reviews.response:
Dear Kai,
Thank you for choosing Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, Curio Collection by Hilton. It sounds like you appreciated our location in the heart of Vegas, but away from the hustle and bustle. Although we apologize for the cleanliness concern, it was our pleasure to remedy this issue. We value you as our guest, and we hope you will stay with us again next time you're in town.

reviews.title: Mother's Day Trip
reviews.text: Nicely redone hotel with spacious rooms. Upgraded with small fee to a suite which was nice. Casino is somewhat sparse and quiet..not a ton of action. Kassi Beach restaurant was very good. Pool area  was very nice however could not bring own water in, and only option was to pay for water at the bar or have it delivered to your chair. The delivery option included an additional fee and this caused a bottle of water to be close to $10 which is outrageous! I also overheard some very young children asking for cups of water at the bar and they were told they would have to pay. Not a great service to guests when spending a day in the sun at over 100 degrees outside.  Most desert hotels at least offer self-serve water stations to keep guests hydrated at the pools.  Other than this, overall our stay was pleasant and I would choose to stay again.
reviews.response:
Dear Shellie,
Thank you for your recent visit with us. We are glad to hear that you enjoyed your accommodations and had a pleasant stay with us, but we’re saddened to hear that the service in the pool area did not meet your expectations. We will use your constructive feedback as a way to continually improve upon the experience of our guests. Thanks again, and we wish you happy and safe travels.

reviews.title: Not for everyone
reviews.text:
I found the price to be pretty high for a “non strip” hotel. This was our first time (couple) in a off strip hotel and we were surprised (some good some bad). 
The good: 
-You get more for your buck. The rooms are basically suites. 
- The in house restaurants are surprisingly really good and “hip”. 
- Nice staff. 
The bad:
- Price. Yes you do get more for your buck BUT rates are pretty much the same as a decent hotel on the stip. 
- Casino. Nothing special about it, pretty depressing to be honest. 
—
Summary: if you plan to be in the hotel for most of your trip and only have destinations scheduled (grabbing an uber to your scheduled events).. we recommend. 
If your plan is to walk all day touring around the strip… we don’t recommend. You’re better off in a Vdara, Planet Hollywood, and such (similar rates).

But generally the whole complex feels empty, not much to do, and quite far from any Las Vegas action. Barely a few tables to play. No real bar, except lobby. Very packed pool. Second pool with a beach club was closed for 4 out of 5 days we were here.

Location-wise is not the best if you aim for a quick access to the Strip, but you are able to find some foods right across the street.

Last but not least, overall cleanliness was okay but since I found scattered fingernails on the carpet inside our room, it significantly drops my rating for this aspect.

I was on holiday with my family and two children. The room was very comfortable, stylish and modern. The light switches were automatic. They delivered the baby crib to our room quickly and without any problems, by the time we got to the room the crib was already inside, plus for the service. It was also very quiet in the morning, we were able to get a good night's sleep without slamming doors and noise from our neighbours.

The downside is the location, although the hotel is close to the strip, it's still a long walk, about 30 minutes in the heat. So I had to take a taxi, which is an unnecessary expense. There's also little of interest inside the hotel, it was boring.
reviews.response:

Thank you for the thoughtful feedback. We’re glad to hear your family enjoyed our stylish rooms and restful atmosphere. We always aim to make your stay at Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, Curio Collection by Hilton as seamless as possible. Please know that your insights are vital to us we continually enhance our guest experience. We hope to welcome you back in the future for an even better time.

reviews.title: Review for Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, Curio Collection by Hilton
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, part of the Curio Collection by Hilton, and had a generally pleasant experience. The hotel features a modern and lively atmosphere with spacious and well-designed rooms. The amenities, including a serene pool area, fitness center, and various dining options, were enjoyable and added to the overall experience.

However, it's important to note that Hilton Gold benefits do not apply to this property, which might be a consideration for Hilton members. The hotel may not be the best choice for families, as it is not particularly kid-friendly. Additionally, noise from adjacent rooms was occasionally noticeable, which could be a concern for light sleepers.

The service was friendly, and the housekeeping staff were generally attentive. While the dining options were somewhat limited at night, the food quality during the day was satisfactory.

Overall, Virgin Hotels Las Vegas offers a vibrant and stylish stay with excellent amenities. Just keep in mind the noise levels and the applicability of Hilton Gold benefits. For those looking for a lively and modern experience away from the main strip, this hotel can be a good choice.
reviews.response: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with us. We're glad to hear you had such a pleasant stay with us at Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, Curio Collection by Hilton, and could enjoy our friendly team, delicious food, and excellent, convenient amenities. That being said, we apologize for any confusion regarding the Hilton Honors program. Please know that while we do participate in this program, benefits cannot be applied to guests who book through third-party sites like Agoda, as we have little control over these reservations. Your feedback is essential in helping us grow as a destination, and we hope to welcome you back soon for another lovely stay.

reviews.negatives: I cant believe the pool hours: 9 to 6. You can barely use it when you are there for a conference. That's RIDICULOUS. Given the heat and sun, the time to use the pools = the evening or early morning. It is also unreal that you can't get shade unless you pay. That's CRIMINAL. Lovely pools tho. Too bad you can barely use them.

Overall good experience but next time I will stay at a hotel on the strip.

reviews.title: Too many purchase sensors in the room
reviews.text:
Nice and clean. But there are too many purchase sensors on the snack bar and bottled water in the room, which makes us feel stressful during our stay. The complimentary bottled water is too small (100-200 ml or so) in volume, while the water with purchase sensors are big and standing in front of the TV with the price tag hidden in the QR code. It’s like a trap when you are thirsty at midnight.

Of course, it shouldn’t cost million dollar for a bottled water. I just would like to express my feelings that the way the hotel put purchase sensors can ruin the comfort of the stay.
reviews.response:

Free parking is a bonus - I had a rental car for part of the trip and when deciding between the similarly priced luxor, Sahara and virgin hotel, the free parking is what swung it for me

Location is about 1.5miles off strip, accessible by bus if you're not driving. If youre not using a car and want to avoid daily Ubers to strip/Fremont then Sahara or luxor might be better options as both are served by the frequent deuce bus.

Overall I'd still rate the stay 5/5 as I knew the location was a bit off strip before I booked it and value wise (I paid £*** for the ruby 2 queen chamber for sun-fri being 5 nights) it's superb

reviews.title: Exceptional Stay: A Review of Virgin Hotels Las Vegas
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at the Virgin Hotels Las Vegas and I have to say that I was thoroughly impressed with my experience. The hotel is conveniently located close to the strip, making it easy to get around and explore all that Las Vegas has to offer.

The rooms themselves were spacious, clean, and modern, with all the amenities you could possibly need. I particularly appreciated the attention to detail in the design, which gave the room a luxurious feel without being too over-the-top.

The casino at Virgin Hotels Las Vegas is also quite impressive, with a wide range of games and activities to choose from. Whether you're a seasoned gambler or just looking for a bit of fun, you're sure to find something to enjoy here.

But what really sets this hotel apart, in my opinion, is the staff. From the moment I arrived, I was made to feel welcome and valued as a guest. Every member of the team I interacted with was friendly, knowledgeable, and eager to help in any way they could.

The only downside to my stay was the fact that they charged for hot water if you wanted to make coffee in your room, which was a bit of a surprise. However, this was a minor issue overall and didn't detract from my overall positive impression of the hotel.

In conclusion, I would highly recommend Virgin Hotels Las Vegas to anyone looking for a comfortable, stylish, and convenient place to stay in Las Vegas. With its great location, top-notch amenities, and outstanding staff, it's a truly excellent choice for anyone visiting this vibrant city.

reviews.title: Staff was rude, room was great except for the TV.
reviews.positives: The room was nice and clean. The hotel was clean and easy to find the theatre venue. I didn't get to use the pool, but the pool area looked nice. I like that there is free parking.
reviews.negatives: The girl at the front dest when I checked in didn't seem to know her job too well, and didn't listen when I spoke and talked over me. I'm hard of hearing so that is very frustrating. When I walked up and gave my name, I gave my last name first then my first name. She said she couldn't find me under that name. Apparently she looked it up by my first name because when I haneded her my phone with the booking info she looked again and said "ohh, it's because they put your last name first". I gave her my information in that order, so apparently she wasn't listening. The Pizza Forte was just as frustrating. I first tried to order online and it wouldn't go through. We went downstairs to order and pick up food and it was completely confusing. There is no direction on where you oder or what to do. I finally got that figured out and one of the "Chefs" asked me to verify one of the items, it's really busy and loud in there so I didn't hear her (like I said hard of hearing) sso I said "I'm sorr, I didn't hear you?" and she looked at me with a very impatient look and then repeated herself. This is the worst experience I have had at that hotel. We also had problems with the TV (or cable) in the room. The picture kept breaking up.

reviews.title: Returing customer, first experience better than the second one.

reviews.negatives: While I know the room I booked was a certain category, I asked if we could get a higher floor or a different room. The answer was a straight "no" due to a sold out night. While I understand the category I paid was the one I got, the hotel was def not sold out (there are no sold out hotels in Vegas, especially during low season), so there was no effort made in this regard, simply a denied request. They provided a $50 credit, which is OK but it forces you to eat there so you can use it. Finally, they had asked me beforehand what was the occasion of my trip and the answer was an anniversary, they set the expectation they'd do something nice for us (so I didnt plan anything), only to find that the staff simply promised and underdelivered.

reviews.title: Ok stay for one night. The room was not what we are used to for a Hilton property. Probably won't choose to stay her
reviews.positives: I liked that it was easy to get to, and the free valet. The bed was very comfortable. I also liked how the light switches were all labeled & the ease of being able to plug in our phones & watches. I also liked how the wifi information was provided on the TV screen and we didn't have to search for it. The separate vanity & mirror was also a nice feature.
reviews.negatives: There was no coffee available in the room, which was very disappointing. There was no 24 hour room service either, which was surprising. We arrived at 8 pm and there was no option to order room service, as only the breakfast menu was available and then not until 6 am the next morning. As we had an early morning flight, it would have been nice to at least be able to order a pot of coffee & muffins. Also, there were only two large towels and for a king room, I expected at least 4 large towels, since there were two people on the reservation & staying in the room. The bathroom set up was a little awkward, with the location of the door, and shower door. It was actually easier to leave the bathroom door open in order to easily open & close the shower door. The toilet took a long time to refill and flush more than once. The shower head, was really high & anyone shorter than 6 feet would not be able to switch it to the wand.

reviews.title: Nice hotel but too focussed on money making
reviews.positives:
- Spacious modern room, and also a very spacious and nice bathroom
- Several restaurants with good food quality
- The staff was friendly and helpful
- Nice outside areas and pools
- Live music in the bar
- Some extra events can be booked. E.g. there was a rock band one night.
reviews.negatives:
- I had picked the hotel because I knew it would be really hot in Vegas and the pool area looked really nice. But it turned out that you could only stay there if you had a lounge chair and there were never enough. So we needed to book a cabana - $150 per day during the week and $300 on weekends. No option for booking per hour, and we did not want to stay so long.
- Cocktails at the pool were expensive, tasted of artificial flavors, and looked very food-colored.
- No free drinking water. Tap water tastes bad. A bottle of water is $8.
- No coffee machine in the room
- The music was very loud everywhere. When we sat in the restaurant, we heard very loud music from inside, very loud music from the pool, and very loud rock music from the event lawn at the same time.
- The air conditioning was set to very low temperatures in the restaurants and the casino. We always needed sweaters. Why would anybody want it extremely cold inside while it is extremely hot outside?
- If you touch anything in the fridge or from the snack tray, you are charged immediately. This is not really clear at first view.
- Also, I was curious about the "Romantic Kid" on the snack tray and read the "ingredients". Turned out it was two condoms and a towel. Guess the price - $69!!
- The light switches were weird - it took me forever to figure out how to get the right lights on in the bathroom.
All in all, the hotel is clearly not 'customer first', but 'make money first'. I would not use it again.

reviews.title: Best value, very clean. Service is exceptional
reviews.text:
Hello, 
I've stayed at the Cosmopolitan, Tuscany Suites, and now Virgin Hotels on my trips to Las Vegas. 
Virgin Hotels is the best value of the 3. I actually like that it's just off the strip because ubers were less expensive, and we rented a vehicle for 3 days so the complimentary valet and free self-park is a huge positive!! 
You can order things for the room through the TV interface and it arrives within a couple of minutes. The staff are kind and helpful, and I won money at their casino! 
We stayed in the Canyons Tower and had a nice spacious suite with a tub and shower. 
The decor and style are modern and refreshing. The view was nice. The dimmable lighting throughout the whole suite is neat! 
We will be coming back here next year <3
reviews.response:
Dear Michael,
Thank you for sharing such a thoughtful review of your stay. It is wonderful to hear that you found such strong value in choosing our property, appreciated the ease of being just off the Strip with convenient parking options, and enjoyed ordering items to your room so quickly through the television. We are very glad that our team made a kind impression, that you had some fun casino wins, and that your spacious suite, with its modern design, tub and shower, and dimmable lighting, felt so comfortable. We are delighted that you plan to return next year, and we look forward to welcoming you back!

reviews.title: Nice Stay - 50/50 We stay there again

We enjoyed not having to walk half a mile to get in and out of the hotel.
reviews.negatives: We didn't like that we couldn't completely open the double doors that opens to about 4" to 5" foot deep balcony. reviews.title: Comfortable accommodation
reviews.text: It is a cost-effective and comfortable accommodation. Although I was given the room at the very end, which required a long walk, and there was no view, only trashy surrounding rooms, the condition of the room was excellent. The free parking is very good. It's nice to have parking lots on both sides, but first-time visitors may find it difficult to locate and access the nearest parking lot. The lifeguards at the pool often enforce rules for children, making it seem more suitable for couples than families. The pool itself is nice.
reviews.response:
Dear Jung,
Thank you for your review and feedback. We're happy to hear that you found the accommodation comfortable and appreciated the quality of the room and the convenience of our free parking. We also understand your concerns about the location of your room and the pool experience, and we'll definitely look into how we can improve signage and better guide first-time visitors to our parking areas. We are sorry the view didn't meet your expectations and that the pool rules made it feel less family-friendly. While we do have guidelines in place to ensure safety for all, we understand the importance of creating a welcoming environment for guests of all ages, and we'll continue to review this balance. We truly value your insights and hope to welcome you back soon.

1. I'm disappointed by the pool security staff. My daughter and my 4 years old son were heading back to the room after swimming, because of the temperature difference between inside and outside of the building and they are still wet. They felt cold, thus I asked for 2 towels to wrap them up. However, I got a cold reply by the security staff: "No! I can only give you one towel. Towels have to stay with the pool and you cannot bring it along to the room". I don't know how big trouble will be causing to the pool staff by bringing the towels to the room, but I know this was not the service should be provided by a so-called 4-star rated hotel.

2. No coffee maker provided, no free space in the refrigerator. And if you move or remove beverages in the fridge it will automatically charge you. Really!?

3. Air ventilation in the bathroom accumulated loads of dust. It seems it hasn't been cleaned for a long time.
